vehicle.label: 6-DOF Industrial Arm
vehicle.desc: 6-DOF serial industrial robot arm, 5kg payload, 600mm reach

parts.label: Base Joint (J1)
parts.spec: Base rotary joint J1, +-180deg, harmonic drive, 100Nm peak torque, mounting flange ISO 9409

parts.label: Shoulder Joint (J2)
parts.spec: Shoulder pitch joint J2, +-120deg, harmonic drive, 80Nm, cable routing through center bore

parts.label: Upper Arm (Link 2-3)
parts.spec: Upper arm link, 300mm length, hollow tube with cable conduit, lightweight Al 7075

parts.label: Elbow Joint (J3)
parts.spec: Elbow pitch joint J3, +-150deg, cycloidal reducer, 50Nm, encoder dual-stack

parts.label: Forearm + Wrist (J4)
parts.spec: Forearm with wrist roll J4, 250mm length, +-360deg continuous, slip ring for power/signal

parts.label: Wrist Pitch (J5)
parts.spec: Wrist pitch joint J5, +-120deg, harmonic drive, 20Nm, compact form factor

parts.label: Wrist Roll (J6)
parts.spec: Wrist final roll joint J6, +-360deg, harmonic drive, 10Nm, ISO 9409-1 50mm tool flange

parts.label: Tool Flange + Changer
parts.spec: Tool changer master plate at J6 end, pneumatic locking, 4 utility ports

parts.label: Cable Management Spine
parts.spec: External cable carrier from base to wrist, drag chain or spiral, signal + power + pneumatic

parts.label: Replaceable Motor Module Set
parts.spec: Actuation_motors closure module: replaceable motor_module cartridges for J1-J6, fastener_access, coupling_dummy, insert/washer preload pads, torque_Nm intent labels

parts.label: Encoder Cover + Sensing Set
parts.spec: Sensing_encoders closure module: encoder_pocket and encoder_cover at each joint, index marks, cable exit strain relief, inspection labels

parts.label: Drive Electronics Bay
parts.spec: Drive_electronics bay with driver_bay, power_channel, signal_channel, ground_point, service cover, connector keying, thermal inspection window

requirements: Shoulder pitch joint J2, +-120deg, harmonic drive, 80Nm, cable routing through center bore

risk.desc: Potential thermal dissipation issues with harmonic drive
risk.mit: Implement additional thermal dissipation fins and conductive paths

risk.desc: Cable routing may cause strain relief issues
risk.mit: Ensure all cable exits have strain relief geometry and proper bend radius

risk.mit: Add stiffening ribs or plates to the base plate

risk.desc: Harmonic drive mount may not be robust enough
risk.mit: Increase the thickness or add reinforcement to the harmonic drive mount

risk.desc: Leveling screws may not provide sufficient adjustment range
risk.mit: Increase the diameter or number of leveling screws

physics_paths.description: Transfer load from base to shoulder link through J2
Pass cable through center bore from shoulder to wrist link
Dissipate heat from motor to housing via thermal conduction
Shoulder pitch motion between shoulder and wrist links
Pressure from motor assembly to housing
Electrical and data signals through cable spine
Access for maintenance and service through shoulder link

integration_opportunities.description: Integrate harmonic drive into shoulder joint for precise motion control
Ensure cable management through shoulder joint to prevent tangling and damage
Implement thermal management solutions to prevent overheating during operation

manufacturing_strategy.process: CNC machining for aluminum parts, LPBF for steel and composite parts
manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary datum is the shoulder link mounting surface
manufacturing_strategy.tolerance: Tolerances are ±0.1mm for critical dimensions and ±0.2mm for non-critical dimensions
manufacturing_strategy.support: Support structures are removed after printing and machining
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Inspection includes dimensional checks and non-destructive testing

verification_focus.description: Verify the mass of the shoulder joint is within the budgeted range
Perform load testing to ensure the joint can handle the specified torque and payload
Test the motion range and precision of the shoulder joint
Conduct thermal testing to ensure proper heat dissipation
Verify the cable routing and harness management through the joint

risk.desc: The bearing seat may not provide adequate support if the fit is not properly specified.
risk.mit: Specify the fit intent and ensure the bearing seat has a shoulder or retaining feature.

verify.rule: mass budget

verify.value: 1. base_plate, 2. shoulder_plate, 3. wrist_plate
verify.rule: assembly_sequence_feasible

verify.value: All parts have a continuous load path to ground
verify.rule: load_path_closure

verify.value: Cable spine is externally accessible or inspectable through open channels
verify.rule: cable_spine_serviceability

verify.value: Joint cartridges can be removed without cutting printed links
verify.rule: joint_cartridge_replaceability

verify.value: Each joint has a bearing seat with shoulder or retaining feature
verify.rule: bearing_shaft_mounting_sanity

verify.value: Harnesses crossing joints have slack loops and strain relief
verify.rule: moving_joint_harness_workmanship

verify.value: Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure and have keying
verify.rule: connector_accessibility_and_keying

verify.value: J1-J6 axis rings are visible
verify.rule: axis_datum_readability

verify.value: Harnesses are routed through visible raceways
verify.rule: cable_harness_visibility

verify.value: Access is represented by geometry such as hatches and pockets
verify.rule: access_not_just_text

verify.value: Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry and routing respects minimum bend radius
verify.rule: harness_strain_relief_and_bend_radius

verify.value: Every inter-part interface names a specific connection type
verify.rule: connection_type_named

verify.value: Parts can be installed in a stated order where every fastener, connector, and mating feature stays reachable
verify.rule: assembly_sequence_feasible

verify.value: One primary datum part anchors the assembly; each other part locates off a named parent datum using a 3-2-1 scheme
verify.rule: datum_chain_continuity

verify.value: The assembly uses the fewest interfaces that still allow service and manufacture
verify.rule: interface_minimization

verify.value: The design moves in the right direction: fewer interfaces, parts consolidated where service is preserved
verify.rule: structural_development_direction

risk.desc: Potential for cable harnesses to become tangled during operation

risk.desc: Risk of cable harnesses being pinched during movement of the arm
risk.mit: Ensure proper slack loops and strain relief at all moving joints

risk.desc: Risk of connectors becoming misaligned or damaged during assembly
risk.mit: Ensure connectors are easily accessible and have clear keying or labeling

risk.desc: Risk of bearing shafts becoming loose or misaligned during operation
risk.mit: Ensure each joint has a bearing seat with shoulder or retaining feature

risk.desc: Risk of cable spine becoming inaccessible for maintenance or inspection
risk.mit: Ensure cable spine remains externally accessible or inspectable through open channels
